content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 12, 1988


Hedy Govenar
Governmental Advocates, Inc.
1127 - 11th Street, Suite 509
Sacramento, Ca 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-068,
Follow-Up

Dear Ms. Govenar:

Following receipt of our advice letter (No. A-86-068), you
have written to request that the Commission amend the term
"lobbying firm," as used in the Political Reform Act,1/ to
allow lobbyists for an organization such as yours to file as a
single lobbying firm. The staff has reviewed your request and
believes the request is a reasonable one which will still
provide the public with the information necessary to satisfy
the purposes of the Act. Accordingly, it is our intention to
present to the Commission a proposal to amend Commission
Regulation 18238.5 which defines the term "lobbying firm." A
draft version of that proposal is attached. I would appreciate
receiving your comments on the proposal as soon as possible.

Assuming the proposal is satisfactory, we anticipate it
will be presented to the Commission at the May 3 meeting. If
the Commission approves the proposal, we will publish notice of
the proposed regulation and will present it to the Commission
for final adoption sometime during the summer.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Hedy Govenar
April 12, 1988
Page 2


     If you have any questions please contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: John G. McLean
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JGM:plh
Attachment

Amend 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18238.5 to read:

18238.5.   Definition of Lobbying Firm; Individual Contract
            Lobbyist

     (a)  A business entity is a lobbying firm pursuant to

Government Code Section 82038.5(a)(2) if it receives or becomes

entitled to receive at least $5,000 in compensation in any

calendar quarter for the purpose of influencing legislative or

administrative action on behalf of any other person, and any

partner, owner, officer, or employee of the business entity

engages in direct communication for the purpose of influencing

legislative or administrative action.

     (b)  The definitions in Section 18239(d) apply to this

section.

     (c)  An "individual contract lobbyist" as used in the

definition of a "lobbying firm" pursuant to Government Code

Section 82038.5, does not include an individual lobbyist if:

          (1)  The lobbyist contracts exclusively with one

lobbying firm;

          (2)  The lobbyist's certification is included

with the lobbying firm's registration statement; and

          (3)  The lobbyist does not file a lobbying firm

registration statement independent of the lobbying

firm.

AUTHORITY:  Gov. Code Section 83112
REFERENCE:  Gov. Code Section 82038.5; 86104


4/11/88                    1                    18238.5A

88-068

GOVERNMENTAL
ADVOCATES, INC.

F P P '.

Legislative Advocacy/Governmental Affairs

FEB 9 8 53 AM '88

February 8, 1988

Ms. Carla Wardlaw, Political Reform Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Carla:

As you suggested in our telephone conversation today, we
are sending this letter to explain how Governmental Advocates
functions with its individual lobbyists.

I am the President, Secretary and Chief Financial Officer
of the Corporation, and its only employed lobbyist. I am also
the only voting Director. The two other Governmental Advocates'
lobbyists are non-voting Vice-Presidents of the Corporation,
and have exclusive contracts to lobby by and for Governmental
Advocates only. They are compensated as contractors and pay
self-employment taxes. However, any lobbying contracts are
done through the entity of Governmental Advocates.

We were advised through the FPPC Seminar and paid profes-
sional consultants, to file as a single firm whose lobbyists
worked for, and were compensated by, that single entity. However,
our recent audit report cites us for failing to file as
separate lobbying firms.

In view of the above, would you please determine for us
the proper filing mode for Governmental Advocates, Inc. and
for each of its lobbyists. If it is determined that we have
been filing incorrectly, we will need considerable technical
assistance to rectify the filings and to segregate the entity's
activities and records into separate filers for future reports.

Thank you for assistance in making clear the purpose, intent,
and implementation of this section of the reporting requirements.

Yours truly,

HEDY GOVENAR

HC:bm

1127 - 11th Street, Suite 509 • Sacramento, California 95814 • (916) 448-8240

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 10, 1988


Hedy Govenar
Governmental Advocates, Inc.
1127 - 11th Street, Suite 509
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  88-068

Dear Ms. Govenar:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on February 9, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice.  If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed.  If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

JP:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

sections.question: In view of the above, would you please determine for us the proper filing mode for Governmental Advocates, Inc. and for each of its lobbyists. If it is determined that we have been filing incorrectly, we will need considerable technical assistance to rectify the filings and to segregate the entity's activities and records into separate filers for future reports.
sections.conclusion: The staff has reviewed your request and believes the request is a reasonable one which will still provide the public with the information necessary to satisfy the purposes of the Act. Accordingly, it is our intention to present to the Commission a proposal to amend Commission Regulation 18238.5 which defines the term "lobbying firm."
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Governmental Advocates, Inc. should file as a single lobbying firm or whether its individual contract lobbyists should file as separate lobbying firms under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC staff found the request reasonable and proposed amending Regulation 18238.5 to allow individual contract lobbyists working exclusively for a single lobbying firm to file as part of that firm rather than as separate entities, with the amendment to be presented to the Commission for adoption.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter (Feb 8), acknowledgment letter (Feb 10), and FPPC response letter (April 12) with proposed regulatory amendment attached. The April 12 letter is the primary advice response indicating FPPC's conclusion and proposed action.

QUESTION: In view of the above, would you please determine for us the proper filing mode for Governmental Advocates, Inc. and for each of its lobbyists. If it is determined that we have been filing incorrectly, we will need considerable technical assistance to rectify the filings and to segregate the entity's activities and records into separate filers for future reports.

CONCLUSION: The staff has reviewed your request and believes the request is a reasonable one which will still provide the public with the information necessary to satisfy the purposes of the Act. Accordingly, it is our intention to present to the Commission a proposal to amend Commission Regulation 18238.5 which defines the term "lobbying firm."

embedding.summary: This document contains FPPC correspondence responding to Governmental Advocates' inquiry about proper filing requirements for lobbyists. The FPPC staff agreed that the company's proposed filing structure was reasonable and committed to presenting a regulatory amendment to the Commission allowing lobbyists to file as a single firm.
